howItWorks.intro: Notary fees are regulated by state law, with each state setting maximum allowable charges per notarial act.
rules: Most states cap fees at $2–$15 per notarization for in-person services
Remote Online Notarization (RON) typically costs $25 per notarization and is legal in most states
Mobile notaries charge travel fees of $25–$100+ on top of per-signature fees
Real estate closings often involve multiple notarizations and may have higher total costs
howItWorks.outro: Some banks and credit unions offer free notary services to account holders. UPS Store, FedEx Office, and AAA offices also provide notary services at or near state maximum rates.

faq.question: How much does a notary cost?
faq.answer: Notary fees range from $2 to $15 per notarization in most states for in-person service. Remote Online Notarization (RON) typically costs $25. Mobile notaries who travel to you charge additional travel fees of $25-$100 or more depending on distance.

faq.question: What is Remote Online Notarization (RON)?
faq.answer: RON allows documents to be notarized via a secure video call. The signer and notary connect through an approved platform with identity verification. RON is legal in most states and typically costs $25 per notarization. It's convenient for people who can't visit a notary in person.

faq.question: Can I get free notary services?
faq.answer: Yes. Many banks and credit unions provide free notary services to their account holders. Some libraries, government offices, and employers also offer free notarization. Call ahead to confirm availability and any document limitations.

faq.question: How much does a mobile notary cost?
faq.answer: A mobile notary charges the state-regulated fee per notarization plus a travel fee, typically $25-$100 depending on distance and time. For after-hours, weekend, or rush requests, expect to pay $75-$150+ in travel fees.

faq.question: Do notary fees vary by document type?
faq.answer: The per-notarization fee is the same regardless of document type in most states. However, real estate documents often require more notarizations (sometimes 5-15 signatures), increasing the total cost. Some states have specific fee schedules for certain document types.

faq.question: What do I need to bring to a notary?
faq.answer: You need the unsigned document (never sign before the notary), a valid government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID), and any required witnesses. The notary will verify your identity and watch you sign. Some documents may require additional forms of ID.

sections.title: What Is the Notary Fee Calculator?
sections.body: The Notary Fee Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals navigating legal issues who need quick, accurate calculations in the legal matters space. By entering your state, document type, number of notarizations, you get instant results including fee per notarization, notarization total, travel / service fee. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting fee per notarization right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In legal matters, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals navigating legal issues worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand your legal situation with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your state and need to find the right fee per notarization. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your State

howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: document type, number of notarizations, notary type. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your fee per notarization, notarization total, travel / service fee. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your state before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.

If you're unsure about your notary type, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
